channel.title: Bread on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Bread on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Traditional Gingerbread Man Cookies
item.description:
Submitted by Stephanie and Shawn Thomas

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 5 to 5 ½ cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 2 tsp ginger
  • 2 tsp cinnamon
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 1 tsp cloves
  • 1 cup shortening
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1 ¼ cups unsulphured molasses
  • 2 eggs, beaten

Instructions

Preheat oven to 375 degrees. Thoroughly mix flour, soda, salt and spices. Melt shortening in large saucepan. Cool slightly. Add sugar, molasses and eggs. Mix well. Add four cups of dry ingredients and mix well. Turn mixture onto lightly floured surface. Knead in remaining dry ingredients by hand. Add a little more flour, if necessary, to make a firm dough. Roll out on a lightly floured surface to ¼ inch thickness for cut-out cookies. Bake on an ungreased cookie sheet (small and medium-sized cookies for 6-10 minutes, large cookies for 10-15 minutes). One recipe of this dough will yield 2 to 3 dozen average-sized cookies.

item.title: Caramelized Pear & Apple Bread Pudding
item.description:
Submitted by Judy and Roger Lafreniere

Aunt and Uncle of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 2 tbsp butter
  • 2 pears, peeled, cored and cut into ¼ inch wedges
  • 2 apples, peeled, cored and cut into ¼ inch wedges
  • ¼ cup brown sugar
  • 5 cups french bread, cut into ½ inch cubes
  • ½ cup raisins
  • ½ cup pecans, coarsely chopped
  • 2 ¼ cups real whipping cream
  • 3 eggs
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 1 tsp cinnamon

Instructions

In a large frying pan, melt butter over medium heat. Add fruit and cook , stirring occasionally until tender. Stir in brown sugar, increase heat and cook for 3 minutes. Remove from heat; stir in the bread, raisins and pecans. Set aside.

item.title: Zucchini Bread
item.description:
Submitted by Patti and Ray Racine

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 3 eggs
  • 2 cups sugar
  • 1 cup oil
  • 2 cups grated zucchini
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 3 cups flour

Instructions

Mix all ingredients together. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes. Makes 2 loaves.

item.title: Banana Bread
item.description:
Submitted by Linda and Mike Gosson

Parents of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 3/4 cups flour
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • 1/4 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1/3 cup shortening
  • 2/3 cup white sugar
  • 2 eggs, beaten
  • 1 cup mashed bananas

Instructions

Blend flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt together. In another bowl, cream shortening and sugar together. Add beaten eggs and blend. Add dry ingredients alternately with bananas.

Beat after each addition until well blended. Pour into a loaf pan and let stand for 20 minutes. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes.  Poke it in the middle and if it’s still doughy-raw, then kick ’er back in for another 5 mins.

item.title: Beer Bread
item.description:
Submitted by Billy Bell

Groom

Ingredients

  • 3 cups self-rising flour
  • 1 tbsp sugar
  • 1 six-pack of beer
  • 1 tbsp butter, melted

Instructions

Preheat the oven to 325 degrees.

In a large bowl, blend the flour and sugar. Quickly mix in one can of beer. Place the mixture in a 9x5 inch loaf pan. Wet your fingers and smooth the top.

Bake for 1 hour or until golden brown. Drink the other 5 beer while you’re waiting.

item.title: Whipped Shortbread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ lb butter
  • ½ lb margarine
  • ½ cup cornstarch
  • 1 cup sifted icing sugar
  • 3 cups sifted flour

Instructions

Cream butter and margarine gradually with cornstarch and sugar. Add flour, a little bit at a time, and whip mixture until it looks like cream. The more they are beat – the better they are.

Drop by spoonfuls on cookie sheet and bake in a 325 degree oven for 22 minutes.

item.title: Never Fail Shortbread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup Canadian Corn Starch
  • ½ cup icing sugar
  • 1 cup sifted all-purpose flour
  • ¾ cup butter (do not substitute)

Instructions

Sift cornstarch, icing sugar and flour together. Blend in butter with a spoon, mixing until a soft, smooth dough forms. Shape dough into 1” balls. Place about 1-1/2” apart on ungreased cookie sheet. Flatten with a lightly floured fork.

Bake in 300 F. (slow oven) for 20 – 25 minutes or until edges are slightly browned.

item.title: English Shortbread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb butter
  • 1 cup fruit sugar
  • 4 cups flour

Instructions

Cream butter. Add sugar. Mix well. Add flour a little at a time, mixing well each time. Roll out ½ inch thick. Cut with cookie cutter. Bake on ungreased cookie sheet at 325 degree oven.

Bake 15 minutes. Turn cookie sheet. Continue baking for another 15 minutes.

Credit to Lorry Bernard.

item.title: Butterscotch Shortbread Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ lb butter
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • 2 cups flour

Instructions

Mix the ingredients together; roll out ½”thick.

Cut out with metal cookie cutters.

Bake 325 F. or 20 minutes on an ungreased cookie sheet.

NOTE FROM COLETTE PEPLINSKIE: I use a pastry cutter, then knead into a ball

item.title: Corn Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup cornmeal
  • ½ cup sugar (use more than this)
  • ½ cup flour
  • 4 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1-1/2 cup milk
  • 1 egg, beaten
  • ½ cup oil or shortening

Instructions

Heat oven to 425 F. Mix corn meal, sugar, flour, baking powder and salt. Add milk, eggs and oil stirring only to mix well. Pour into a 9” baking dish or casserole. Bake until light brown or about 25 minutes. Test till it comes out clean.

item.title: Wild Rice Many-Grain Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup wild rice
  • 2 Tbsp granulated sugar
  • ½ cup warm water
  • 1 pkg dry yeast (or 1 Tbsp )
  • 5 cups (approx.) all-purpose flour
  • ¼ cup fancy molasses
  • 2 Tbsp shortening
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ½ cup natural bran
  • ½ cup raw sunflower seeds
  • ¾ cup 7-grain cereal
  • ¾ cup rolled oats

Instructions

  1.  In saucepan, cook wild rice in 2 cups water (500 ml) water for about 45 minutes or until tender; drain and let cool.

  2.  Meanwhile, dissolve 1 tsp (5 ml) of the sugar in warm water. Sprinkle in yeast; let soften, stirring together until dissolved. Stir in ¼ cup (50 ml) of the flour to make a thin paste; set in warm place for 10 minutes or until bubbly.

  3.  In large bowl and using electric mixer, combine molasses, remaining sugar, shortening, salt and 2 cups (500 ml) warm water. In order, beat in bran, sunflower seeds, yeast mixture, wild rice, 7-grain cereal and rolled oats. With wooden spoon, beat in as much of the remaining flour as necessary to make firm dough. Turn out onto lightly floured surface; knead for about 10 minutes or until smooth and elastic, kneading in as much of the remaining flour as necessary..

  4.  Place in large greased bowl, turning to grease all over. Cover with plastic wrap; let rice in warm draft-free place until doubled in bulk, about 2 hours. (NOTE: I tried the plastic wrap and found the dough stuck to it even if it was lightly greased; I choose instead to make sure that there is flour on the surface and then cover it with a clean cloth.)

  5.  Punch down dough; turn out onto lightly floured surface. Divide into thirds. With rolling pin, flatten each to about 7-1/2-inch (19 cm) square; firmly roll up each into log. Place each in greased 8- x  4-inch pans. Cover and let rise until doubled in bulk, about 1-1/2 hours.

  6.  Bake in center of 375 F (190 C) oven for 15 minutes or until loaves sound hollow when tapped on bottom. Remove from pans; let cool on racks. Makes 3 loaves, 12 slices each.

item.title: Water Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup lukewarm water
  • 1 tsp sugar
  • 3 cups (2 boiling hot & 1 cold) water
  • 2 tbsp sugar
  • 1 tbsp salt
  • 1 tbsp dry yeast or 1 envelope
  • 7 – 8 cups all-purpose flour     (6 measured out to start with, then technique determines the rest - see below)
  • ¼ cup shortening or margarine

Instructions

Pour 2 cups boiling water on salt, 2 tbsp sugar and shortening in bowl.  When shortening has melted, add 1 cup cold water.  Prepare yeast (see NOTE below).

item.title: Toasty Garlic Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 French bread, 12 to 15”, cut into 1”cubes
  • ½ cup butter or margarine, softened
  • Parsley flakes
  • Grated Parmesan cheese
  • Garlic powder

Instructions

Spread both sides of bread with butter. Place, cut side down, on a cookie sheet. Sprinkle one side with cheese, parsley and garlic powder. Broil 4 inches from the heat until light golden brown. Turn slices and repeat with other side. Serve immediately.

item.title: Raisin Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup lukewarm water
  • 1 pkg or 1 Tbsp yeast
  • 1 tsp sugar
  • 3 cups tepid water (2 boiling, 1 cold)
  • ¼ cup shortening
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 2 tsp cinnamon
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 2 beaten eggs
  • 1 cup lightly floured raisins
  • Flour

Instructions

Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Dissolve sugar and yeast in 1stquantity of water

Melt ½ cup sugar, shortening and salt in 2 cups boiling water, then cool mixture with the one cup of cold water.

item.title: Old-Fashioned Bread Dressing
item.description:
Old-Fashioned Bread Dressing (Stuffing)

Ingredients

  • ½ cup chopped onion
  • ½ cup butter or margarine
  • 1 tsp poultry seasoning
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1/8 tsp pepper
  • 8 cups dry bread cubes1 cup chicken broth or water

NOTE: (I (Mom) use 1 tsp chicken bouillon powder in 1 cup boiling water.)

Saute onions in margarine. Add all other ingredients and mix well. This amount stuffs a 10-lb turkey or can be heated in a greased foil-covered bread pan in a 325 degree oven for 40-45 minutes if it has been refrigerated, or at a lower temperature for a longer period.

item.title: Magic Sweet Yeast Bread
item.description:
Combine in a cup:

  • 1/2 cup lukewarm Water
  • 2 tsp sugar

Sprinkle over the top:

  • 2 pkg dry yeast

Let soften for about 15 minutes.

Scald in a saucepan over boiling water:

  • 2/3 cup Milk

Mix in & Set aside to cool:

  • ½ cup St. Lawrence Oil
  • ½ cup Bee Hive Corn Syrup

Measure and sift together:

Ingredients

  • 5-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ¼ cup sugar

Instructions

Combine with the milk, corn oil and syrup mixture …5 beaten eggs and 2 cups of the sifted flour, sugar, salt mixture. Mix to give a thick batter. Beat vigorously and stir in yeast.

item.title: Cranberry Christmas Gingerbread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup sifted all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp ground ginger
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ½ cup butter, softened
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 1/3 cup whole-berry cranberry sauce
  • 1 egg
  • ½ cup unsulfured molasses
  • ½ cup boiling water

Instructions

Grease and flour an 8” x 8” baking pan. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Sift together flour, baking soda, ginger and salt. Set aside. Using an electric mixer in a large bowl, cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy.

item.title: Corn Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup flour, sifted
  • ½ cup yellow corn meal
  • 3 tsp baking powder
  • 1 cup milk
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 egg, beaten
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 1 Tbsp melted shortening

Instructions

Mix and sift flour, baking powder, salt and sugar. Stir in corn meal. Add milk to beaten egg and stir into first mixture. Add shortening and blend. Turn into shallow greased 8-inch pan. Bake at 400 degrees for 20 minutes.

item.title: Cheese Bread
item.description:
Credit for this recipe goes to Table Treasures.

Ingredients

  • ½ cup chopped onion
  • 1 Tbsp butter
  • 1 egg
  • ½ cup milk
  • 1-1/2 cup Bisquick
  • ½ cup grated cheese

Instructions

Saute onion in butter. Mix egg in milk. Add Bisquick and grated cheese, then onion. Stir only until mixed well. Pour into buttered 1-1/2 quart pan. Top with more cheese or poppy seed if desired. Bake at 400 F. for 20 – 25 minutes.

item.title: Banana Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 bananas, mashed
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 egg, beaten
  • 1-1/2 cup flour
  • 1 tsp each baking soda, baking powder
  • 1/8 tsp salt
  • 2 tsp melted butter or oil
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Mash bananas and mix with white sugar. Add beaten egg. Sift together flour, baking soda, baking powder and salt. Add to first mixture. Beat well. Add melted butter and vanilla. Bake in a greased loaf pan at 350 degrees for 35 to 45 minutes. Test to make sure it is baked right through.

item.title: Spinach Bread Dip
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 10-oz pkg spinach
  • 1 cup mayonnaise
  • 1 cup sour cream
  • 1 pkg dry Knorr vegetable soup mix
  • 2 green onions
  • 1 small can water chestnuts

Instructions

Cook spinach just lightly; drain and chop. Chop onions and water chestnuts. Mix all ingredients together and serve with small pieces of bread.

NOTE: You can use a large round load of pumpernickel bread. Cut bread from the center and use this to serve your dip. The bread taken from the center is used as small pieces to dip.

item.title: Shortbread Cookies #2
item.description:
• 1 1/4 cups all purpose flour
  • 1/2 cup cornstarch
  • 1/2 cup icing sugar
  • 1 cup butter, softened
  • pinch of salt

Preheat oven to 275 degrees Fahrenheit.

In a medium bowl sift together cornstarch, icing sugar, and flour. Add butter; stir until smooth dough is formed.

Line a cookie sheet with parchment paper. Roll dough into 1-inch balls. Place two inches apart on cookie sheet. Flatten with your fingers or fork. Decorate with seasonal sprinkles.

item.title: Shortbread Cookies #1
item.description:
• 1 cup soft butter
  • 1 1/2 cups flour
  • 1/2 cup cornstarch
  • 1/2 cup icing sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla or almond flavouring

Roll out or cookie press. Decorate with small pieces of red and green cherries. Bake in 325 degree Fahrenheit oven for around 15-20 mins.

NOTES:

Ingredients

  • I used margarine once and butter the next. MUCH better with real butter.
  • 4 out of 5

Credit to Camp Lau-Ren’s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook

item.title: Dutch Oven No-Knead Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 1/2 cups warm water (about 100 degrees F)
  • 1 packet yeast (active dry, instant, or quick rise- 2 1/4 teaspoons)
  • 1 ½ tsp salt
  • 3 1/4 cups all-purpose flour

Instructions

  1. In a large bowl(or container), mix together the warm water(1 1/2 cups), yeast(1 packet/2 1/4 teaspoons), and table salt(1 1/2 teaspoon), until yeast and salt are fully or almost dissolved(I like using a whisk for this).
  2. Add the flour(3 1/4 cups)to the bowl all at once and stir together until a sticky dough forms. Don’t worry about mixing it too much, just make sure everything is uniformly wet. It WILL be messy and sticky. You can scrape what’s left on the spoon with a silicone spatula.
  3. Cover the bowl with a kitchen towel(or place the container to the lid on ajar).Leave it for 2-3 hours to rise in a somewhat warm place until it’s doubled in volume.
  4. 30 minutes to an hour before you are ready to bake, preheat your oven to 450 degrees F. Place your Dutch oven in the oven, with the lid ON, while the oven is preheating. Once the oven reaches 450, keep it preheating for another 20 minutes, so the Dutch oven gets good and hot.
  5. Scrape the dough into the edge of a piece of parchment paper dusted with flour. Make it into as much of a “loaf” shape as you can by folding the edges up on top of it(a silicone spatula works well for this).
  6. Then, use the edges of the parchment paper to flip the loaf over so the floured side is on top and the loaf is in the center of the parchment paper. Don’t worry about it looking beautiful or smooth on top. Also doesn’t matter if your dutch oven is round or oval.
  7. Use a very sharp knife or clean razor blade to cut a few slits across the top of the loaf about 1/2" deep, across the entire top. I did this in a cross shape for mine.
  8. Take out the preheated Dutch oven and take off the lid, carefully! Grab the parchment from the sides and place the loaf in your Dutch oven.
  9. Place the cover back on the Dutch ovenand place in the preheated oven on the center rack. Bake for 35 minutes at 450 degrees F. Remove the lid and bake for another 5-10 minutes, until a golden brown color forms- longer if necessary. Remove the loaf from the dutch oven(grabbing the sides of the parchment paper works well here)and place it on a wire rack or cutting board to cool for at least 10 minutes(preferably 30 minutes – an hour, if you have time)before slicing.

Billy’s Notes:This was really good!  Pretty easy too.  Takes some time, but pretty easy to make if you need a declicious and fancy-looking loaf of bread in a pinch.  Like Mom’s recipe, I chose to butter the top of my loaf when I pulled it out of the oven and it softened it up and made it even more delicious-er.

item.title: Bread Pudding II
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 slices day-old bread
  • 2 tablespoons butter, melted
  • 1/2 cup raisins (optional)
  • 4 eggs, beaten
  • 2 cups milk
  • 3/4 cup white sugar
  • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

Billy’s Note:I didn’t have raisins once, so I subbed in 2 apples cut up in little pieces.  I drizzled caramel sauce on it to make a caramel apple bread pudding.  Worked great!

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
  2. Break bread into small pieces into an 8 inch square baking pan. Drizzle melted butter or margarine over bread. If desired, sprinkle with raisins.
  3. In a medium mixing bowl, combine eggs, milk, sugar, cinnamon, and vanilla. Beat until well mixed. Pour over bread, and lightly push down with a fork until bread is covered and soaking up the egg mixture.
  4. Billy’s Note:I let it soak up all that eggy milkness for around 15 mins here before putting it in the oven.
  5. Bake in the preheated oven for 45 minutes, or until the top springs back when lightly tapped.

Credit for this recipe goes to AllRecipes.com.  Here is the actual link to their page:

item.title: Keto Cheesy Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese
  • 1/2 cup shredded parmesan cheese (use 1 cup if you really like cheese)
  • 1 egg
  • 1 1/2 tsp garlic powder
  • Pinch of Italian seasoning
  • Parchment paper
  • Salt

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 F.
  2. Mix all ingredients together in a bowl and pat it out like you would pizza dough into parchment paper (not wax!)
  3. Sprinkle with some salt.
  4. Bake for approx. 15 minutes.
  5. Broil on high for 1-2 minutes to get a crisper top and brown edges.
  6. Cut with a pizza cutter and dip in some Raos marinara sauce!

Billy’s Notes:Just sent this one my way, so I haven’t tried it yet. She said it’s great though!

item.title: Cloud Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 eggs, room temperature
  • 3 tbsp cream cheese, softened
  • 1/4 tsp cream of tartar
  • 1/4 tsp salt (Billy’s Note: Maybe cut even this in half - I found them salty)

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 300℉ and line two baking sheets with parchment paper.
  2. Carefully separate egg whites from yolks. Place whites in one bowl and yolks in another.
  3. In the bowl of egg whites, add cream of tartar and salt. Using a hand mixer, mix together at high speed until stiff peaks form.
  4. In the bowl of egg yolks, add cream cheese and mix together with a hand mixer until well-combined.
  5. Pouring slowly, use a spatula or spoon to add yolk mixture to egg whites and carefully fold in until there are no white streaks.
  6. Spoon mixture onto prepared baking sheet about ½ to ¾ inches tall and about 5 inches apart.
  7. Bake in the oven on middle rack for 30 minutes, until tops are lightly golden brown.
  8. Allow to cool (they will likely be too crumbly directly out of the oven) and enjoy.

Billy’s Notes:These were decent, but they’re VERY airy and light. Good for a cold sandwich, like meat, but gets soggy with something like a fried egg. Note that there are a lot of interesting variations to these to jazz them up a bit. Some pizza ones, “everything bagel” ones, etc… Once I experiment with them, I’ll note them here.

item.title: Chaffle "Wonder" Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 12 Tbsp of egg white (this is equal to 4 Large eggs)
  • 4 heaping tsp of Mayonnaise
  • 4 Pinches of salt
  • 4 Tbsp of cream cheese (softened - can just microwave for a few seconds)
  • 2 tsp of baking powder
  • 1 tsp of sweetener (Stevia)
  • 8 Tbsps of almond flour
  • 2 tsp of psyllium husk
  • 2 tsp of water (if necessary - should be like a pancake batter consistency - I didn’t need to when I made this)

NOTE: I quadrupled this because the original recipe only makes 2 of them. The recipe measurements above will make 8 of them.

item.title: 90-Second Keto Bread in a Mug
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 tablespoon butter
  • ⅓ cup blanched almond flour
  • 1 egg
  • ½ teaspoon baking powder
  • 1 pinch salt

Instructions

Microwave butter in a microwave-safe mug until melted, about 15 seconds. Swirl the mug to coat with butter.

Add almond flour, egg, baking powder, and salt to the mug; whisk until smooth.

Microwave on high until set, about 90 seconds. Let cool for 2 minutes before slicing.

Credit for this recipe goes to AllRecipies.com:
